heroHeadline: Committed to safe, reliable, and sustainable power system analysis solutions
heroSubtext: Delivering innovative power system solutions by integrating advanced technology and strategic expertise.
heroImageAlt: Carelabs engineering team reviewing power system studies at a US facility
missionBody: Helping you transform your power systems from a cost center into a competitive advantage. Since our inception in 2015, CareLabs has committed to fostering a safety culture within organizations. Over the years, we have successfully partnered with many industries in Canada to help them accomplish their safety goals. Our core values align with the requirements of our clients and the standards of the industry we serve. As we look ahead, we envisage pioneering electrical safety and compliance, empowe

values.title: Safety First
values.description: Every decision we make — from how we scope a project to how we write a report — is evaluated against the question: does this make workers safer? Safety is not a department or a policy at Carelabs; it is the reason we exist.

values.title: Technical Excellence
values.description: Electrical safety engineering is a discipline where errors have serious consequences. We hold ourselves to the highest standards of technical accuracy, using industry-leading ETAP software, current IEEE and NFPA standards, and peer review on every project.

values.title: Client Partnership
values.description: We build long-term relationships with our clients, not transactional engagements. We invest in understanding your facility, your operations, and your compliance challenges so that our studies are actionable and our recommendations are practical.

values.title: Innovation
values.description: The power systems and electrical safety landscape is constantly evolving. We stay ahead of changes in IEEE 1584, CSA Z462, CCOHS regulations, and protective relay technology to ensure our clients always benefit from the most current engineering knowledge.

team.bio: Dr. Mitchell is a licensed Professional Engineer with 25 years of power system engineering experience. He holds a PhD in Electrical Engineering from Ontario A&M University and is a member of the IEEE Power & Energy Society and the NFPA Technical Committee on Electrical Safety in the Workplace. He has led over 500 arc flash studies and power system projects across North America.
team.name: Dr. James Mitchell, PE

team.bio: Sarah Chen is a licensed Professional Engineer and Certified Fire and Explosion Investigator with 18 years of experience in electrical safety and power system analysis. She specializes in arc flash hazard mitigation and has developed electrical safety programs for clients ranging from community hospitals to Fortune 100 manufacturers. Sarah leads Carelabs' team of 15 engineers and project managers.
team.name: Sarah Chen, PE, CFEI

team.bio: Marcus Johnson is a licensed Professional Engineer specializing in protective relay coordination and power system protection with 20 years of experience. He holds certifications in multiple protective relay platforms and has extensive experience with modern digital relays, IEC 61850, and arc flash detection systems. Marcus leads Carelabs' relay coordination and protection practice.
team.name: Marcus Johnson, PE
